subject: REVERSAL: prompt enhancement is BACK IN for Krea2 — in-workflow toggle, not Cubric Prompt. Supersedes the 'branch CUT' decision in both your handoffs.
body:
## READ FIRST — this overturns a decision you are currently holding

Both open MPI-242 handoffs (554f1336…, 7b3e4c91…) carry this in `context.key_decisions`:

> "Prompt enhancement branch CUT from the app workflow (user tested Comfy's TextGenerate: over-expands, drifts from intent). Cubric Prompt is the app's answer to enhancement."

**That decision is REVERSED as of 2026-07-10, by the user, in a live session.** Prompt enhancement ships IN the Krea2 workflow. Do not treat this message as stale — it is NEWER than the handoff you loaded. Cubric Prompt is deferred; this unblocks the one feature that actually needed it.

The original drift complaint is not disputed and not resolved — see "Open risk" below. It is accepted as a tuning problem, not a blocker.

---

## What to build

A **boolean toggle** in the prompt-box controls (you are already in that file for the style controls — same surface, same pass).

- Toggle OFF (default): today's behaviour, prompt → Krea2 text encoder, unchanged.
- Toggle ON: prompt → `TextGenerate` → expansion → Krea2 text encoder (positive conditioning).

This is **one workflow**, not a second graph and not a pre-pass. No extra model load. No per-model recipes. Explicitly rejected by the user: a separate "enhance" button that runs its own graph, because it would force loading an LLM before the diffusion model and make the user wait twice.

### Persistence semantics (user-specified, matches how hosted gen sites behave)

1. User writes a short prompt, toggles enhancement on, hits Generate.
2. **The prompt box does NOT change.** The user keeps seeing their own text.
3. What is saved to the sidecar is the **expanded** prompt — that is what the encoder actually saw.
4. **Reuse Prompt** on a history card returns the **expanded** prompt, not the original.

### Sampling

`sampling_mode: "on"`, ComfyUI defaults (temperature 0.7, top_k 64, top_p 0.95, min_p 0.05, repetition_penalty 1.05). Non-deterministic on purpose — press again, get a different expansion, keep the one you like. The expansion is persisted as an artifact at generation time, so there is nothing to reproduce and **no seed is exposed or saved** (this is consistent with the standing no-seed-UI rule, not an exception to it).

### Scope of THIS phase

- Krea2 only. Text-only. No image input to the enhancer, no captioning, no video, no audio.
- Do not attempt to generalise to Wan/Chroma. See "Why only some models" below — it is not a policy choice, it is a hard capability limit.

---

## The technical facts you need (verified against ComfyUI source, not docs)

Node: `TextGenerate`, display name **"Generate Text"**, core ComfyUI, `comfy_extras/nodes_textgen.py`. Already present in the pinned engine — **no version bump required** (confirmed: it is in the shipped frontend nodeDefs, and used by the Ernie / StableAudio blueprints in `engine/ComfyUI_windows_portable/`).

The node body is ~3 lines of real work:

```python
tokens = clip.tokenize(prompt, image=image, skip_template=not use_default_template,
                       min_length=1, thinking=thinking, video=video, audio=audio)
generated_ids = clip.generate(tokens, do_sample=..., max_length=..., ...)
return clip.decode(generated_ids)
```

It does **not** contain an LLM. It runs the LM head of **the text encoder you already loaded**. For Krea2 that encoder is Qwen3-VL-4B — a decoder LLM that ComfyUI normally only taps hidden states from. Hence: zero extra VRAM, zero extra weights, zero extra download, no new dependency, no `add-model-playbook` treadmill for this feature.

### Why only some models get this (do NOT generalise)

Eligibility = does the loaded CLIP implement `.generate()`. Static per-encoder fact. If it does not, the node raises `AttributeError` — it does not degrade gracefully.

| Text encoder | `.generate()` | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Qwen3-VL (Krea2) | YES | our case |
| Gemma3-12B (LTX-2) | YES | ships its own `TextGenerateLTX2Prompt` w/ tuned T2V+I2V system prompt |
| Gemma-4 (E2B/E4B/31B) | YES | + video + audio. Would be a NEW model load — that is Cubric Prompt's problem wearing a hat. Rejected. |
| T5 / umT5 (Chroma, Wan) | **NO** | node CRASHES. Never wire it. |

Relevant classes: `comfy/text_encoders/qwen3vl.py::Qwen3VLClipModel.generate`, `krea2.py::Krea2Tokenizer` (inherits Qwen3VL), `llama.py::BaseGenerate`.

### Media-input truth table (the official docs are WRONG here)

`TextGenerate` exposes `image`, `video`, `audio` sockets on **every** CLIP. They are forwarded as kwargs into `clip.tokenize()` and each tokenizer decides what to honour. **Unhandled modalities are silently swallowed — no error, no warning.**

- Krea2 / Qwen3-VL: honours `image` ONLY. `video` and `audio` are **ignored** (they fall into `**kwargs` and die in `SDTokenizer`). See `qwen3vl.py:161` — it only reads `kwargs.get("image")`.
- Gemma-4: honours all three.

So: do not wire the video/audio sockets for Krea2. They look functional and do nothing.

### Templating / the system-prompt seam — THIS IS THE IMPORTANT PART

`use_default_template=True` wraps the prompt in the model's chat template. For Qwen3-VL that is:

```
<|im_start|>user\n{prompt}<|im_end|>\n<|im_start|>assistant\n
```

…plus, when `thinking=False`, an appended `<think>\n\n</think>\n\n` to force an empty reasoning block (Qwen3 convention — inverted from what the name suggests).

**There is no system role in that template.** The official Krea2 graph works around this by hand-rolling a system prompt into the *user* turn via a `Text String` + `Concatenate Text` rig.

**Escape hatch:** if the prompt string *starts with* `<|im_start|>`, the tokenizer sets `skip_template = True` and passes your raw string through untouched (`qwen3vl.py`, the `text.startswith('<|im_start|>')` check). So a real system role is available by constructing:

```
<|im_start|>system\n{SYSTEM_PROMPT}<|im_end|>\n<|im_start|>user\n{user_prompt}<|im_end|>\n<|im_start|>assistant\n
```

This is exactly the mechanism `TextGenerateLTX2Prompt` uses for LTX (it builds a `<start_of_turn>system…` string for Gemma). **Krea2 has no equivalent node — we must own the system prompt string ourselves.**

---

## Open risk — the reason this was cut the first time

The user's recorded finding was: **over-expands, drifts from intent.** That is an output-quality problem and it is NOT solved by this message.

Strong hypothesis, unverified: **the drift was the default template, not the model.** A naked `<|im_start|>user\n{prompt}` gives the LLM no instruction to stay faithful, so it free-associates. The Krea2 reference workflow's own system prompt is a 9-rule instruction sheet built to prevent precisely this — its rule 1 is *"Faithfulness First: Preserve all original subjects, actions, colors, and spatial relationships. Do not add new objects, props, characters, or animals unless the user clearly implies them"* and rule 5 is *"Avoid Over-Specification."*

**Therefore: the system prompt IS the feature.** Do not ship this with `use_default_template` and no system prompt — that reproduces the exact failure that got it cut. Build the `<|im_start|>system…` string, put the faithfulness rules in it, and expect to iterate on the wording with the user. Treat prompt-string tuning as the deliverable, not the node wiring (the wiring is trivial).

If the drift persists *with* a faithfulness-constrained system prompt, escalate to the user before sinking more time — that would mean 4B is genuinely too weak and Cubric Prompt was right after all.

---

## Checks before you call it done

- [ ] Toggle OFF is byte-identical to today's graph (no stray node, no changed conditioning).
- [ ] Toggle ON: sidecar stores the EXPANDED prompt; prompt box still shows the user's original.
- [ ] Reuse Prompt on a card built with the toggle ON returns the expanded prompt.
- [ ] Works on BOTH engines. The remote/Pod path is a separate code path and this repo forgets it every single time (MPI-122/163/164/216/222/229/231/237 all bit on exactly this). Enhancement is a graph submission like any other, but verify the Pod actually has the node and the expansion round-trips.
- [ ] Node naming law: any non-Tier-1 node we add must be `Input_*` / `Output_*` (MPI-116).
- [ ] Measure the latency of the enhance step (512 autoregressive passes through a 4B model) and confirm it is disclosed to the user as a generation-time cost. The user explicitly accepts a slower gen for better output, but it must be opt-in and it must not look like a hang.

## Sources

- `comfy_extras/nodes_textgen.py` (node + the LTX2 system prompts, verbatim, worth reading as a model for ours)
- `comfy/text_encoders/qwen3vl.py`, `krea2.py`, `gemma4.py`, `llama.py::BaseGenerate`
- `comfy/sd.py:458` (`CLIP.generate`)
- https://docs.comfy.org/built-in-nodes/TextGenerate (thin, and wrong about media inputs)

provenance.reverses_decision: Prompt enhancement branch CUT from the app workflow (recorded in handoffs 554f1336-a18c-4484-b3e2-eed004dc9173 and 7b3e4c91-2a58-4d17-8e63-1f9a05c7d284)
provenance.rationale: TextGenerate runs the LM head of the ALREADY-LOADED Krea2 text encoder (Qwen3-VL-4B). Zero extra model load, so the cost argument that favoured deferring to Cubric Prompt does not apply. Cubric Prompt deferred.
